title: [Feature Request] Option to align/match Y-axis width
body:
I would love to have the Y-axises' width aligned on multiple graphs. 

For example on the below screenshot, I have 2 graphs with information about a given humidity sensor, and at the bottom a graph that shows the speed of my ventilation. However, the different type of axis data cause the axises to have a different width. This makes it harder to compare the graphs because they are basically shifted and stretched compared to each other.

![grafana_alignment](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1453214/14010150/f38ebb74-f192-11e5-8b0b-df49c5196a9c.png)

I'm running versoin 2.6.0

I like to be able to use athena as a datasource for sql queries.

athena user guide http://docs.aws.amazon.com/athena/latest/ug/what-is.html

title: [Feature request] Email templating in Alert engine
body:
**- What Grafana version are you using?**
4.0.1
**- What datasource are you using?**
OpenTSDB
**- What OS are you running grafana on?**
CentOS 7.2


I would like to congratulate for including alerting feature in Grafana 4, this is just what I needed to create alerts for things that our monitoring did not cover. To make email alerting more flexible I would propose to create simple mail templating engine:

**Current Use Case**
We setup alert thresholds and alert recipient as well as static text which informs about the cause, incomplete Graph (no text, no legend) is also added

**What is missing?**
However I think that the email engine lacks templating option (or documentation on this feature if it is possible). What I would like to request is:
- formatting the metric value with units or just manipulate it via variable
- ability to use some HTML tags / BB code / custom grafana rules for text formatting (at least support for generating links)
- use of dasboard-specific variables such as query alias, graph title, dashboard title or template variables defined in dashboard (could be used as a temporary workaround since alerts do not work with template vars in queries), date of alert triggering - this is mainly for customizing links which Grafana could generate when alert occurs
- Create PNG from the graph state when alert triggered - currently no text is drawn onto the graph making it quite unreadable - exact PNG image from configured view would have been sufficient if possible
- Subject templating - simple textbox with variable support

Pros:
- make alerts customizable, lazy users will not be forced to create new mail filters
- make messages clear to understand - at least by formatting it to the unit used in graph - for instance in rates measured in byte difference but displayed in Mbit/s

Cons:
- can't think of any, except somebody has to write some code and docs on it

Keep up the good work !

title: Feature Request: add support for error bars in graphs

Please include this information:

- What datasource are you using? InfluxDB

Please add support for error bars in graphs. The ability to show configurable size intervals from standard deviation and number of samples (i.e., get these two numbers from additional fields in the point)  would be great.

title: [Feature] Row collapse status in URL
body:
Some large dashboards have lots of rows collapsed by default:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/89186/44884572-e69a8100-ac70-11e8-9f77-1e72b070bddf.png)

Each row itself has lots of graphs and it's often not needed to unroll them by default.

When people look at these dashboards and want to sow multiple graphs, they have to send a link and tell which row to unroll. This is not very usable.

I suggest that row collapse status is passed into URL, so when shared, people see exactly what the person sharing the dashboard wanted to show.

title: [Feature request] playlist provisioning
body: I'm using the feature of provisioning dashboards and datasources. These are good for deploying to kubernetes. I want to use provisioning playlist too 😄

title: Support Backend plugins for Alert notifiers
body:
**What would you like to be added**:
The ability to create custom Notification Channel Types -- similar to a datasource plugin -- to support custom notification platforms.

**Why is this needed**:
We have a use case for our own internal notification framework interfacing with grafana alerts. In this case, a simple webhook would not provide the level of integration desired to fully utilize the framework.
We would want to create a "plugin" that would allow for us to specify configuration settings -- ones that are unique to our framework -- when creating a new notification channel.

title: Generic HTTP CSV / TSV datasource plugin
body:
Timesires data is often stored in TSV or CSV files that slowly get appended to in cases where databases are not setup.

It would be great to be able to pull data such as this into grafana.

I started working on a datasource plugin for such a case https://github.com/addshore/grafana-tsv-datasource

This currently allows you to specify a TSV file over HTTP and load it into grafana.
It supports different column layouts so long as there is a date that can be parsed by JavaScript and a value in the mix.
It also supports files with and without data headings.

I open this for general tracking and to let people know there is some code that can kind of do this now!

title: Feature Request: Expose API for generating CSV files from Graphs
body:
I would like to request an API to create CSV files from a graph in Grafana.  

Grafana Version: v3.1.0
Datasource: Graphite
OS: Linux

I work for a mobile gaming company and we are using Grafana to track certain metrics in our game.  We currently want to export a csv file to parse through and create an automated report of our findings.  The way Grafana is set up now, we'd have to manually download the CSV file each time we want to generate this report.  We plan on creating this report fairly frequently, either daily, weekly, or even after a new build.  If we had an API exposed to us to generate this report automatically, we could automate the creation of the report much more easily.  

I posted about this in the community forums:
[https://community.grafana.com/t/api-to-generate-csv-file/590]

Daniel Lee suggested I create a feature request here.  
Hopefully there's enough interest to create this feature.  Thank you.

-Brandon Chew

title: Graph: timeshift per query
body:
**What would you like to be added**:
It would be nice to display different timeranges on single graph panel.
Currently it is available only on 2 separate panels like this:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19311511/77226912-26d79200-6b7c-11ea-8591-4a204ad02730.png)
To make it on one graph panel, timeshift needs to be set differently for each query.

**Why is this needed**:
Ability to make comparisons between different timeranges on single graph.

title: [Feature request] Option to Disable Getting Started Panel
body:
Hi,

We're deploying Grafana as part of an application stack. Part of our deployment procedure is to load dashboards into Grafana. Our deployments are ephemeral (changes are lost when the stack is rebuilt) but we configure our instances with no-auth and with the default role as Admin so that our users can adjust/tune graphs before exporting them and rolling them back into config-management.

The "Getting Started" panel adds confusion for our users since it implies some action is required, when it isn't. Would it be possible to make the panel configurable through the config file?

Thank you kindly,

Grafana version: v4.3.0-beta1 (commit: 3a89272)
Data source: prometheus
OS: Linux

body:
The point of monitoring and alerting is to detect and act,
I think it would be nice to have an option to run scripts from grafana, or attach a script to a custom button,
perhaps a console that can save the commands to create a script.

title: alert support dynamic threshold or variable in condition.
body:
<!-- Please only use this template for submitting feature requests -->

**What would you like to be added**:
alert support variable in condition.

**Why is this needed**:
I need alert when one point's fieldA `IS ABOVE` fieldB. but now alert just support fixed threshold.

that is, alert condition support `dynamic threshold` or `variable`.

title: Use Slack threads for resolved and repeated alert notifications
body:
**Why is this needed**

To keep a good overview of triggered alerts in Slack channels and stay well informed about and focussed on issues that are still happening.


**What would you like to be added**

It would be very nice if a feature is added to the Slack alert contact point that makes it possible to:

- Have the resolved message sent in the Slack thread of the first message about a firing alert.
- Have the repeated messages sent in the Slack thread of the first (original) message about a fired alert, and sent as a reply in the channel (possibly as a separate option, that gives the possibility to choose if the repeated message should also be sent in the channel). Slack has the option to send a message in a thread and also make it directly appear in the channel (checkbox _also send to <channel>_/_also send as direct message_).

It would be an absolute bonus if the colour of the vertical line in front of the first (original) message changes to green when the alert/notification is resolved.


**Who is this feature for?**

Users who use Slack to get notified about firing alerts.

title: Legend: Add default sort options (similar to tooltip)
body:
A common feature request is adding the ability to set a default sort option for the legend (specifically for the timeseries panel but this could also apply for other panel types)

[See this user's more than reasonable feature request](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/30907#issuecomment-1680559308) :)

Investigate adding default sorting options in legend settings. We should also consider the graph render behavior (whether or not to support sorted legend order to impact series order)

Related to https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/18186

title: [Feature Request] Support LDAP Configuration through Environment Variables
body: LDAP configuration (as of Grafana v3.1.0) must be loaded from a file and cannot be read from environment variables. However, file-based configuration can complicate Docker based deployments, especially in cloud deployments such as Kubernetes or OpenShift. Ideally, LDAP configuration would be configurable using environment variables just like most other Grafana configuration settings.

title: Interpolate tag value in alerting message
body: **What would you like to be added**: Is it possible to add label interpolation to tag value? **Why is this needed**: This feature will help filter alerts by tags based on variables. It's a killer feature for using with tools like Opsgenie and others.

title: Switch between alternative panel views
body: **Why is this needed**: Information related to the same subject sometimes need to be represented in multiple views. For example, I want to plot CPU usage of my applications on the dashboard. CPU usage can be represented in absolute and relative values. In order to see if my applications are close to CPU saturation I'd plot it with relative % usage: If I see that application is close to the limit, I'd need to drill into absolute values: I could do this by just having two or more panels on the dashboard. But, I do not always need to see absolute value. So these extra panels would take screen space and distract user's attention. **What would you like to be added**: Please, add an option to have an alternative view for a panel which can be toggled by user on demand: Clicking on alternative view would switch panel query&settings to alternative ones, allowing user easily to switch between the views. Currently, to let users see an alternative view I'm using data links functionality: But it is not obvious for users that they could click on the line and be taken away to a new page with alternative panel view. Please note, relative/absolute views is just a demonstrative example. Having a panel toggle to show all services or only outliers is much more useful application for this feature. **Who is this feature for?** This feature is for devops and software engineers who rely on Grafana dashboards for monitoring and troubleshooting.

title: Silence button in alert list
body:
**Why is this needed**:

To mute alerts directly from the alert list.

In the `/alerting/groups` view, a silence button is present, making this unintuitive and confusing for users.

**What would you like to be added**:

A "Silence" button should be present for alerts in `/alerting/list`, at least for firing alerts.

This was present in the past and removed with #62133.
It was reported as missing in #67192, however that issue was closed as "Works as intended".

**Who is this feature for?**

Everybody dealing with alerts, so that they can silence alerts from the Alert list, not only the Grouped list.

title: Make Grafana more friendly to read-only MySQL database
body:
### What Grafana version are you using?
5.2.2
### What datasource are you using?
Mixed (irrelevant to my question)
### What OS are you running grafana on?
Linux
### What did you do?
Grafana is one of the main tools for troubleshooting during outages and we are looking to a way to make it as much reliable as possible.
We have a big installation of Grafana (around 90 instances in the same Domain/Org) 
We are using MySQL to store both Grafana config and Sessions.
We are using MySQL Master->many_Slaves replication chain managed by Orchestrator https://github.com/github/orchestrator 
Since Grafana can utilize only single MySQL server(Master) for it internal needs #399 #3676 we are providing service discovery by using short living DNS records pointing to a current Master, updated by Orchestrator for redundancy. This setup work fine until recently.
The recent DNS outage proved that this DNS dependency is better to be avoided, as If DNS fails then we do not have a way to discover MySQL and Grafana fails to perform any operations without MySQL available. Essentially if your DNS is broken, then everything else is broken.

We were thinking about adding some extra fail-over logic on Grafana boxes where we could pull all available slaves and make them available locally for grafana, for instance via /etc/hosts. This should make grafana available for troubleshooting, however grafana just can't deal with read-only database.

### What was the expected result?
I would expect it to keep providing read-only access to all dashboards, search and navigation should work.

### What happened instead?
 If restarted it can't start 
t=2018-09-24T13:43:23+0200 lvl=eror msg="Server shutdown" logger=server reason="Service init failed: Datasource provisioning error: Error 1290: The MySQL server is running with the --read-only option so it cannot execute this statement"

 If IP of the MySQL has been changed to RO then it can't search for dashboards and navigation is impossible with "Server error" or "Unknown error"

 Proper solution as I can see it: 
- Teach Grafana to work with Write / RO connections separately
- Fail gracefully if Write connection unavailable

title: [FeatureRequest] QueryVariable: Add support for multiple label name values
body:
<!-- Please only use this template for submitting feature requests -->

**What would you like to be added**:

**Why is this needed**:

When I'm using Grafana templating: say I have:

```
{
        "allValue": null,
        "current": {},
        "datasource": "Prometheus",
        "definition": "",
        "hide": 0,
        "includeAll": false,
        "label": "Service",
        "multi": false,
        "name": "service",
        "options": [],
        "query": "label_values(destination_service)",
        "refresh": 1,
        "regex": "",
        "skipUrlSync": false,
        "sort": 0,
        "tagValuesQuery": "",
        "tags": [],
        "tagsQuery": "",
        "type": "query",
        "useTags": false
      },
   ```

I can only query the label values for only one label, here is `"query": "label_values(destination_service)",`, well, I did not find any supports for label_values() to return list of values from multiple label names. Here is the current support I could find: https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/datasources/prometheus/#query-variable

- If there is any logic operator there, "AND" or "OR" to help me combine two label_values() returns into one, like: "query": "label_values(label1) AND label_values(label2)" ?
- or adding support `label_values(label1, label2 ...)` to return label values for multiple label names. As this is pretty convenient when we have multiple labels, those are only for sub-component usages, but at some cases we do not have to or it's not necessary to differentiate them from a global perspective.

title: Limit parameter for "hover tooltip" settings (feature request)
body:
Hi,

I have graphs with high number of monitored objects and there is a nice parameter in graph settings - "Hover tooltip", which has sort order. It would be great to have "limit" parameter which can be useful in case when objects too much and user want to reduce their number in the tooltip.

![grafana-screenshot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/953312/42736066-c335a0bc-8878-11e8-943a-f605fef9ec11.png)

In my case, I will be very happy if grafana able to reduce a list of object to 10, for example.
Please, add "limit" parameter to "hover tooltip" settings.

p.s.: grafana-5.2.1-1, prometheus datasource, centos 7 linux.

title: [Feature Request] Colorize table rows by hidden column value
body:
It is currently possible to set a table column as "hidden".

But a hidden column cannot be used to set the color of a table row based on the value in the hidden column. This means adding tables showing something like a service or node's "health" doesn't work - i.e. where you'd like to display the name of service, and it's color based on a health metric value (i.e. 1 or 0) but not the redundant 1 or 0 value in a separate column.

title: Show series with string values in graph tooltip
body:
I have an InfluxDB query that returns two series, one of which is a numeric metric of an event and another one which is a string that uniquely identifies the event. I understand that the second series cannot be used as a source for any kind of graph, but is it possible to allow showing its values at least on a tooltip over the appropriate point? In 5.4.2 it is just empty.

![tooltip-string](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12149233/55639304-bd773b80-57d1-11e9-80e8-48361b097111.png)

title: Request Enable/Disable Panel Option
body:
Hi, I'm new to the whole Grafana scene. As such, I'm also playing around with different settings, different panels with different queries, trying various things. 
However, one thing I have noticed is there isn't a way to "disable" a panel. Just make it so that the code within doesn't run.
I'm working on a Power monitor setup, I've built the pcb, coded the arduino, and configured influxdb to received the data through a python script.
So while working with Grafana, I'm finding a need to disable panels as I'm stumbling to get things working. However, I think this would be a good function to have in general, as some panels don't "always" need to be active. 

Granted, I haven't gone through all the help files and such, but I am a fan of "Find/Search", and I haven't found a way to "disable", yet. Unless it's called something else, maybe(?)

Currently running Grafana v5.2.4 on an older laptop, a Dell Inspiron 630, unfortunately. The data will be ran on a real server later on. I just need it to be a bit mobile, atm.

Thanks for the great work you guys have done with this project. A year ago I didn't know about Grafana, but I've seen some really nice designs and data displays using it  :)

Kori

title: [Feature request] Embed mode: Add parameter to disable editing
body:
- What Grafana version are you using?: 4.1
- What datasource are you using? Graphite, InfluxDB, Elasticsearch
- What OS are you running grafana on? RedHat 6.8
- What did you do? Embed a panel into Icingaweb2 per performance metric
- What was the expected result? Only graph viewing capabilities, not editing
- What happened instead? Clicking the head of the panel opens the edit bar and you can do the usual actions like view, edit, ...

```
<iframe src="https://ourdomain.com/grafana/dashboard-solo/db/icinga2-graphiteplugin-metric-link?from=1486185993015&to=1486207593015&var-METRIC=icinga2.our_host.services.Login.selenium_host_login.perfdata.loginTime.value&theme=light&panelId=1" width="450" height="200" frameborder="0"></iframe>
```

We would need another parameter in the url like "&editing=false" which prevents all manipulation of the panel/graph and only allow zooming/viewing.
It isn't possible for us to change privileges of the anonymous user as we have an external auth system (based on headers) needing it this way.

Using the builtin dashboard options "Editable" we get pretty close but it would also be great to disable the clickable header.

Currently it looks like this in icingaweb2:
![2017-02-04 14_04_40-icinga web](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/124109/22618672/094c2812-eae3-11e6-9618-ddf94a42f012.png)

title: Enhanced Dashboard Management: Library Rows in Grafana
body:
**Why is this needed**:
Rows play a crucial role in organizing content on dashboards, and it's common to reuse the same rows across multiple dashboards. Introducing a 'library version' of rows would greatly reduce the time and effort needed for maintenance and streamline the distribution of new panels.

**What would you like to be added**:
Option to create 'Library Rows' in a way that's similar to how we currently create 'Library Panels'.

**Who is this feature for?**
This feature is intended for people who develop and/or maintain Grafana dashboards.

title: Timepicker lost moving left / right with relative timespan
body:
### What Grafana version are you using?
just updated to 5.3.0

### What datasource are you using?
InfluxDB

### What OS are you running Grafana on?
CentOS 7

### What did you do?
Opened a dashboard that shows data from the last 6 hours

### What was the expected result?
Grafana 5.2.x had buttons to move left and right in time.

### What happened instead?
They are gone in version 5.3.0

Commit [154fbe2](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/commit/154fbe2413f9abe33157baa563e42fbf67e2f27b#diff-1c0b3228f2e2e7fa2fce6cea8e4f8e92) added `ng-if="ctrl.isAbsolute"`. This is unfortunate because I used moving left / right to get from the last 6 hours (relative) to an absolute time range.

title: "Hide time picker" also hides refresh button
body:
### What Grafana version are you using?
v5.1.0

### What datasource are you using?
InfluxDB

### What OS are you running grafana on?
CentOS 7

### What did you do?
Tried to hide the time picker

### What was the expected result?
The users shouldn't be able to select a time range because that dashboard only uses panels with time overwrite and shows the current value. Refreshing makes sense because the current data changes by definition.

### What happened instead?
The refresh button is also hidden

### Use case
Having a dashboard showing the current status, maybe an average over the last hour.

title: Thresholds: add labels to thresholds
body:
<!-- Please only use this template for submitting feature requests -->

**Why is this needed**:

I'm making a dashboard with thresholds that indicate my goals for each quarter. I'd like to label those thresholds with the name of the quarter, so that users know what they represent. Without those labels, users don't know what the thresholds indicate and I have to explain it in a text panel or panel description.

There's a workaround where I can define an actual series with the right name, but that doesn't quite fit the use case. I also used [value mappings](https://bi.grafana-ops.net/d/30NB_xnVz/grafana-goals-formerly-grafana-10-goals?from=2024-01-31T00:00:00.000Z&to=2025-01-31T23:59:59.000Z&editPanel=555&tab=queries) to do this to some extent, but it would be nice to still see the numbers on the axis and see the threshold values on the right side of the chart.

Ideally I'd like to see the thresholds in the legend, and perhaps labeled on the graph next to the line.

**Who is this feature for?**

A product manager making a business chart, where the meaning of the thresholds can't be communicated with just their colors.

title: CloudWatch logs does not support creating template variables
body:
<!-- Please only use this template for submitting feature requests -->

**What would you like to be added**:
Add support for creating template variables using CloudWatch Logs . Either a VariableQueryEditor needs to be set to some component or a special query language needs to be supported like with metrics https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/features/datasources/cloudwatch/#query-variable

**Why is this needed**:

title: Request: Support provisioning teams through API
body:
<!-- Please only use this template for submitting feature requests -->

**What would you like to be added**:
Extend provisioning API to able to provision teams.

**Why is this needed**:
Related to #12119, but team provisioning would provide an automated fine grained control within the same org.

title: Folder expansion options
body:
Love the new folder and teams additions.

Once thing I'd like to see is some mechanism to allow created folders to be expanded by default when the dashboard list is brought up, to make quick mouse navigation easier.  I think there are several possibilities:

1) Preserve the collapsed/expanded state of each folder shown (per user).
2) Allow for a default collapsed/expanded setting for each folder.
3) Auto expand the folder that the currently viewed dashboard is in.

I imagine there are other options.  From what I've seen so far, "general" seems to be permanently expanded, and only "recent" and "starred" preserve state.

Thanks for listening... (and for Grafana 👍 )

-James

Please include this information:
### What Grafana version are you using?
5.0.3

title: Alias PromQL metrics for Google Prometheus (Stackdriver) datasource
body:
<!-- Please only use this template for submitting feature requests -->

**Why is this needed**:

Currently, there's no way to alias metrics. It makes the legend very long and unreadable.

Interestingly, it's possible to alias in the Builder mode (see pic).


**What would you like to be added**:

We should be able to alias metrics as for Prometheus datasource using `{{label}}` syntax.


**Who is this feature for?**

I work at a company where we want to use PromQL and have our graphs clean.

![image](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/assets/107541/26ba160f-bd3c-4885-a833-d7f4868fcf95)

![image](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/assets/107541/91702e0b-4c70-423c-ae32-bbab671f2083)

title: Alerting: Threshold does not support multiple queries like classic condition does
body: ### What happened? I attempted to replace classic condition with a reduce -> threshold set of expressions as that seems to the proper way to do things in Unified Alerting but there is no way to have a threshold expression apply to multiple queries like you can with a classic condition. This image is the type of expression that I am trying to replace, the queries are InfluxDB queries to different clusters, so replacing them with a single query is not an option. I understand that I cannot use classic conditions any more as they strip out the labels returned by the queries, so my templates become useless without reduce -> threshold. Threshold even says that it supports multiple time series in the editor. ### What did you expect to happen? I expected to be able to set thresholds for multiple datasources as you can with classic conditions. You can only have a single Alert condition so multiple threshold expressions is not an option. ### Did this work before? Yes, it works with classic conditions but this is no longer supported and classic conditions clobber all the labels that are needed to make useful templates. ### How do we reproduce it? 1. Have multiple queries in an alert 2. Attempt to have multiple thresholds ### Is the bug inside a dashboard panel? _No response_ ### Environment (with versions)? Grafana: 9.5.1 OS: CentOS 7 Browser: Google Chrome 114.0.5735.198 ### Grafana platform? A package manager (APT, YUM, BREW, etc.) ### Datasource(s)? InfluxDB

title: Missing an info about building packages
body:
**What would you like to be added**:

An info about building binary packages.

**Why is this needed**:

After removing grunt `go run build.go build package` doesn't work any more and an info about developers/users can build their own packages is missing.

title: Transformations: Row Reduction Field Selection By Regex
body:
<!-- Please only use this template for submitting feature requests -->

I would like to be able to select which fields to perform an "add field from calculation" transformation on by using a regular expression:

example:   Add field from calculation -> Mode: Row Reduction -> Field Name: /my_stat_(\d+)/

this is required to assist with dynamic templated panels where there are too many fields to select individually or the field names change depending on the query and template variables.

title: [New Data Source] Apache Pinot
body: Apache Pinot is a realtime distributed OLAP datastore, which is used at LinkedIn, Uber, Microsoft, Slack and Weibo to deliver scalable real time analytics with low latency. for more details: https://pinot.apache.org/

title: [Feature request] Add stack/unstack toggle to panel menu
body:
Please include this information:
- What Grafana version are you using? 4.6.3
- What datasource are you using? Prometheus
- What OS are you running grafana on? Ubuntu 16.04

Currently if I want to switch between stacked and unstacked mode for graph panel I have to edit the panel and toggle "stacked" box there.
It would be useful to have stack/unstack toggle next to each panel for easy way to change mode.

Justification:
 *  both stacked and unstacked modes have advantages and often neither is clearly better than the other
 * sometimes it's not clear whether lines are stacked or not, I need to add "stacked/unstacked" to panel title
 * ability to quickly toggle mode would save some users from creating duplicated panels
 * it would allow anonymous users to switch panel mode. Currently only logged in users have this ability

I think implementation could be similar to one for https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/9722

title: [Feature Request]: Graph series overrides, fill below to & use regex capture group
body:
* **I'm submitting a ...**
* [ ] Bug report
* [x] Feature request
* [ ] Question / Support request: **Please do not** open a github issue. [Support Options](http://grafana.org/support/)

Let's say you have several servers, each with `last smth` and `first smth` metrics. Trey are presented with two queries but result on 2*n graphics. You may be interested in stacking or filling them without defining a rule for each server. For example:

```json
"seriesOverrides": [
{
"alias": "/last (.*)/",
"fillBelowTo": "first \\1",
"lines": true
}
],
```

Unfortunately this doesn't work even with direct panel `json` insertion.

title: Add option to disable short share links via grafana.ini
body:
<!-- Please only use this template for submitting feature requests -->

**Why is this needed**:

Have option to disable short share links would be useful for stateless Grafana setups, as there no place to persist such links in memory.


**What would you like to be added**:

New settings in grafana.ini that would disable short links option that create links like /goto/short


**Who is this feature for?**

To restrict usage of short links

### Discussed in https://github.com/grafana/grafana/discussions/67978
Originally posted by **ghost** December 20, 2017 Please add desktop notifications in Grafana. This will make it a much better tool for using as monitoring our servers and application. If this will be added we can use it in our production environment.

title: Support color blind single stat thresholds
body:
This is not an issue of something that is broken, but rather something I just realized.

When building a singlestat panel the provided colors for thresholds are red, yellow and red. This makes sense to the vast majority of people, and it should stay this way.

But, I remember once building in a system that had to provide some form of feedback to people to let them know when things weren't working fine. We used colors, like everybody else does, but then one person in the organization approached to me to explain that he was color blind and that what I was doing was basically useless for him, because he saw no difference between them. I was humbled by this simply because I didn't even consider this to be a thing (the blessings of being in the majority side, I guess).

I took a look at the issue tracker and community and failed to find anything about color blind support.

So, I was thinking that grafana could do something really simple to support this: use stripes, solid, or dots, or whatever it makes sense, but have a way of differentiating thresholds by something else than the color itself.

Something as simple as this, with a tick for "enable color blind" would include all this people who we (at least I) don't usually consider.
